name: ADM Ministry
privacyPolicy:
Privacy Policy for ADM Ministry

Effective Date: 01 March 2026

1. Introduction

ADM Ministry Platform is dedicated to protecting the privacy of our members, staff, and leadership. This policy outlines how we collect, store, and secure your personal and spiritual data.

2. Information We Collect

We collect information necessary to facilitate church operations and communication:

  • Identification: Full name, gender, marital status, and baptism records.
  • Contact: Phone numbers and email addresses for ministry alerts and OTP verification.
  • Ministry Data: Assigned branches, departments (e.g., Choir, Ushers), and leadership roles.
  • Financial Records: Tracking of tithes, offerings, and pledges for ministry accounting.

3. Data Usage & Security

Your data is used strictly for internal ministry purposes, such as attendance tracking and financial reporting. We implement the following security measures:

  • Encryption: All passwords and sensitive tokens are cryptographically secured.
  • Access Control: Data is only visible to authorized personnel like Pastors and Treasurers.
  • Infrastructure: We use secure hosting and trusted SMS gateways for all communications.

4. Your Rights & Account Deletion

You have the right to review your data or request its removal. To permanently erase your profile and deactivate your account, please use the Account Deletion tool found in the app settings or on this compliance hub. Once authenticated, your personally identifiable information will be removed from our active databases.

5. Contact Information

For questions regarding your data, please contact your local Branch Administrator or the Ministry Head Office.
terms:
Terms of Service

Last Updated: March 2026

1. Agreement to Terms

By accessing the ADM Ministry Platform, you agree to abide by these terms. This platform is provided for the sole purpose of supporting church administration and member spiritual growth.

2. User Accounts & Security

You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your login credentials. Any activity performed under your account is your responsibility. You must immediately notify an Admin if you suspect unauthorized access.

3. Code of Conduct

Users must interact with the platform in a manner consistent with ministry values. Unauthorized attempts to access data outside of your assigned role or branch are strictly prohibited.

4. Administrative Responsibilities

Leaders and Administrators (Pastors, Treasurers, etc.) agree to handle member data with the highest level of confidentiality. Misuse of financial records or personal member information is grounds for immediate termination of access and potential disciplinary action.

5. Service Limitations

We strive for 100% uptime but do not guarantee that the service will be uninterrupted. We reserve the right to modify or discontinue features necessary for the security and efficiency of the ministry.
